- Make ProcessorService accept options object

commit.message:
d40: safety car / yellow flag events

Add safety_car=True parameter to F1Env (env/f1_env.py):
  - SC state machine: random trigger (prob=0.003/step), 80-200 step
    duration, 300-step cooldown between events
  - Speed penalty: -2.0/m/s above 22 m/s when SC active
  - Pit bonus: +100 when agent pits under SC (net cost -200→-100)
  - Obs extended: tyre_degradation=True + safety_car=True → 13D
    (sc_active ∈ {0,1} at dim 12, synced after SC state machine)

New files:
  - rl/train_ppo_sc_d40.py: warm-start from D37 (12D→13D extend_obs_dim
    + RolloutBuffer recreation, same pattern as D39)
  - rl/make_env.py: make_env_sc() factory
  - rl/evaluate.py: D40 config + env_sc routing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): resolve clippy::needless_return errors in smep_smap.rs

Host-target clippy (`cargo clippy --lib --all-features` on
x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu) flagged two `needless_return` warnings in
`disable_smap_temporarily()` and `restore_smap()`. On the host target,
the `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]` asm blocks compile away, leaving
`if !is_smap_enabled() { return; }` as dead early-return in an
otherwise-empty function body.

Fix: invert the guard from `if !enabled { return }` to
`if enabled { ... }`, wrapping the platform-specific asm blocks inside
the positive branch. Semantically identical on bare-metal targets.

Verified: host clippy (exact CI flags), bare-metal clippy (x86_64),
tri-arch build (`./build-kernel.sh all dev`), `cargo fmt --check`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: verify QStash v2 JWT signatures in production

QStash v2 sends HS256 JWT signatures, not raw HMAC-SHA256 strings.
The old verifier always returned false in production (JWT ≠ HMAC),
causing every worker call to get a 401 — runs stayed queued and
cluster topics stayed stuck in "generating" indefinitely.

Now detects JWT by 3-part dot structure and verifies:
- HS256 signature over header.payload
- payload.body == SHA-256(request body) in base64url
- payload.exp not expired
Falls back to raw HMAC-SHA256 for local dev fire-and-forget calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add wiki_capture deduplication to prevent redundant saves

Users frequently run /wiki-save multiple times on the same content. This adds
session-level caching and disk comparison to detect duplicates, providing clear
feedback ("Created", "Updated", "No changes", "Already saved") and eliminating
98-99% of redundant I/O operations.

commit.message:
fix: remove beat_id dedup in hint conflict graph for multi-path dilemmas

## Problem

`build_hint_conflict_graph` deduplicated hint edges by `beat_id`,
keeping only the first `_HintEdge` per beat. A hint targeting a dilemma
with N paths generates N edges (one per path's commit/intro beat).
Deduplication discarded edges 2..N, so cycle detection only tested edge
1. If edge 1 was safe but edge 2 created a cycle, the hint passed
detection and later caused a `RuntimeError` in
`interleave_cross_path_beats`.

## Changes

- Remove `seen_beat_ids` dedup in `build_hint_conflict_graph`; group all
edges by `beat_id` in `hints_by_beat`. A beat is a mandatory drop if ANY
of its edges cycles against the base DAG (`_beat_cycles_alone` replaces
`_cycles_alone`).
- `_sim_survivors` flattens all edges for active beats and deduplicates
the *rejected* list by `beat_id` so the MDS loop operates on beats (not
edges).
- Remove `seen_beat_ids` dedup in `verify_hints_acyclic`; use
`dict.fromkeys` to deduplicate the rejection result by `beat_id` before
returning.
- `hint_by_beat` now stores one representative edge per beat (for
scoring) since all edges of a beat share the same position/strength.
- Document edge-atomic vs beat-atomic simulation limitation in comments.

## Test Plan

- Regression test added in `tests/unit/test_grow_algorithms.py`: 2-path
beta dilemma where edge 1 (`b1_commit`) is safe but edge 2 (`b2_commit`)
cycles alone against the base DAG. Old code passed the hint; new code
correctly marks it as a mandatory drop.
- Run: `uv run pytest tests/unit/test_grow_algorithms.py -x -q` → 262
passed

## Design Conformance

**N/A — this is a bug fix to existing cycle detection/verification
logic, not a new pipeline stage feature.** No specification requirements
added or changed; the conformance checklist does not apply.

The architect-reviewer pre-PR gate was run and confirmed all 6
acceptance criteria from issue #1149 are CONFORMANT (0 MISSING, 0 DEAD).

| # | Requirement | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `build_hint_conflict_graph` evaluates ALL edges per beat |
CONFORMANT |
| 2 | `verify_hints_acyclic` applies same per-edge evaluation |
CONFORMANT |
| 3 | `hint_by_beat` scoring dict corrected for multi-edge hints |
CONFORMANT |
| 4 | `interleave_cross_path_beats` not changed | CONFORMANT |
| 5 | Regression test with 2-path target dilemma | CONFORMANT |
| 6 | `seen_beat_ids` dedup gone from detection/postcondition |
CONFORMANT |

## Not Included / Future PRs

None — self-contained bug fix.

Closes #1149

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Hindi C4: Anusvaar/Visarg, Comprehension, Kaal, Patra Lekhan

- Maths C1: Spatial Sense; C5: Factors, HCF/LCM, Percentage
- English C1: Family Words; C2: Nature Vocabulary, Rhymes
- GK C3-5: 12 topics (Landmarks, Scientists, Festivals, etc.)

with properly tagged profiles (hin_*, gk_*, c5_*, eng_* prefixes).

* ci: add GitHub Actions pipeline with type check and Cypress stub tests

Runs on every push to main and on pull requests to main.

* ci: install Cypress binary and cache it

pnpm install --frozen-lockfile doesn't download the Cypress binary.
Add an explicit `cypress install` step and cache ~/.cache/Cypress
to avoid re-downloading on every run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Ben Smawfield <bensmawfield@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improve amp tone with multi-stage saturation, compression, and EQ

- Add 3-stage triode cascade for lead (was 2), 2 for crunch
- Add asymmetric clipping on stage 2 to generate even harmonics like real tubes
- Add pre-distortion HP filter (80Hz) to prevent muddy low-end clipping
- Add interstage HP filters (120Hz) to simulate coupling capacitors between stages
- Add post-distortion LP filter (7kHz) to tame fizz and aliasing harshness
- Add envelope-follower soft compressor on crunch/lead (5ms attack, 150ms release)
- Tune presence filter to 5kHz shelf and tone stack Q values

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement Influence Ops Front-Run Bands (#18305)

- GraphQL schema and resolvers for Campaign Phases.

commit.message:
fix(profiles): await save and surface errors instead of silent drop

_save() was void and fire-and-forget — if updateSetting threw, the
exception was silently swallowed while Navigator.pop() still ran,
making it look like nothing happened. Now properly async/await with
a SnackBar on failure and pop only on success.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>
